description:
You are a developer cleaning up a tiny project’s documentation.  
A “docs” folder already exists containing three Markdown files.  
Your job is to:

1. Create a **gzip-compressed tar archive** of the entire “docs” directory.  
   • Name the archive exactly “docs_backup.tar.gz”.  
   • Store it **inside** “/home/user/backups/”.

2. Extract that archive into a brand-new directory named  
   “/home/user/restore/docs_restored”.

3. Create a plain-text log file “/home/user/backup_log.txt” that records what you did.  
   The log file **must match this exact 7-line format** (each line ends with a newline):

CREATED: /home/user/backups/docs_backup.tar.gz  
EXTRACTED_TO: /home/user/restore/docs_restored  
CONTENTS:  
README.md  
architecture.md  
user_guide.md  

Automated tests will check:  
• that /home/user/backups/docs_backup.tar.gz exists and is a valid gzip-compressed tar containing the three original Markdown files under the docs/ prefix;  
• that /home/user/restore/docs_restored/ contains exactly those three files;  
• that /home/user/backup_log.txt matches the 7-line template above **character-for-character** (including capitalisation and blank lines).  
No root privileges are required. Typical commands: 3–4 (tar, mkdir, echo/cat >).  
Work entirely from the terminal inside /home/user.
truth:
Initial filesystem (before the agent starts):

/home/user/docs/README.md
  └─ contents: "# Documentation\n\nThis is the README.\n"

/home/user/docs/architecture.md
  └─ contents: "# Architecture\n\nSystem overview.\n"

/home/user/docs/user_guide.md
  └─ contents: "# User Guide\n\nHow to use the system.\n"

/home/user/backups/            (empty directory, already created)
/home/user/restore/            (empty directory, already created)

Expected final state when the task is done correctly:

1. Archive:
/home/user/backups/docs_backup.tar.gz
  • A valid gzip-compressed tar archive.
  • Tar layout (when listed with tar -tzf):
      docs/README.md
      docs/architecture.md
      docs/user_guide.md
  • Each file’s payload matches the original contents byte-for-byte.

2. Extracted directory:
/home/user/restore/docs_restored/README.md
/home/user/restore/docs_restored/architecture.md
/home/user/restore/docs_restored/user_guide.md
  • File contents identical to originals.

3. Log file:
/home/user/backup_log.txt  (exact 7 lines, final newline)
Line-by-line:
1  "CREATED: /home/user/backups/docs_backup.tar.gz\n"
2  "EXTRACTED_TO: /home/user/restore/docs_restored\n"
3  "CONTENTS:\n"
4  "README.md\n"
5  "architecture.md\n"
6  "user_guide.md\n"
7  "\n"   ← blank line

Permissions: all files/dirs readable & writable by user.
name: task_000000_be5a5c3c
